ocr.text: In the study of hydra morphology and vitality, the data presented in Table 1 provides critical insights into the relationship
between genetic markers and phenotypic outcomes. The scores assigned to various specimens underscore the variability in
morphology, particularly among the normal hydra population. The use of the forward and reverse sequences, specifically
fnVNTR-1 and V-CAGCTAGAAGCTTATTCGCCTCTT, illustrates the genetic framework underpinning these observations.
Score  Morphology  Vital state
7  Normal hydra  Active
6  Bumped gastric region, normal tentacles  Active
5  Inverted-cone gastric region; shortened tentacles  Whitening, gastric region paralyzed, slow tentacles
4  Inverted-cone gastric region, short tentacles  Paralyzed, rigid tentacles
3  Gastric region reduced in size, crushed tentacles  Permanently damaged to death
2  Further reduced gastric region, without tentacles  Abundant cell loss
1  Irregular shape  Death
0  Dispersed cell clumps and debris  Disintegrated
Marker  Primer sequencea  Primer concentration
Forward  Reverse
fnVNTR-1  V-CAGCTAGAAGCTTATTCGCCTCTT  CAGGGTAATGCCTTAACGCATAT  0.2 μM
fnVNTR-2  F-GAGTATTCCCTGCACCTACAATGAT  TGTCATGCCTTTTTCTCTAGAGGAT  0.2 μM
fnVNTR-3  N-CAAACTCTTTCACAAGAGGAAGCAT  TCTGAACTCTGCTCTTTTCCCTCTA  0.2 μM
fnVNTR-4  F-TTGTGCAAACACATCGATAGGAGAT  AACTGCATCATCAGCATCTCTTCTA  0.2 μM
fnVNTR-4b  GTGCTTTTGCCTGTACCACCTT  GCATCATTTGATACACCGTCCA  NA
fnVNTR-5  N-CAATCACTCATCAACCACTAGCCAT  TGCTGACTAATGGCTGACTGTAGTT  0.3 μM
fnVNTR-5b  CTCATCAACCACTAGCCATCACAT  AAAGCCTGGGCTATCTAAATGCT  NA
The analysis focuses on a concentration of 0.2 μM, which has been identified as optimal for eliciting measurable responses in
the vital state of the hydra. The correlation between the morphology scores and the vital state of the specimens suggests that
genetic factors play a significant role in determining the health and robustness of these organisms. Such findings may have
broader implications for understanding the genetic regulation of morphology and vitality in aquatic invertebrates. The
meticulous recording of the morphology and vital state metrics serves to enhance the reliability of conclusions drawn from this
research, paving the way for future studies aimed at exploring genetic influences on organismal development and adaptation in
diverse environments.
